title: DOM Manipulation
content:
## 🌳 DOM Manipulation: Making Pages Dynamic The **DOM (Document Object Model)** is the browser's in-memory, tree-structured representation of an HTML document. JavaScript can read and modify it in real time — adding, removing, and changing elements and their content. --- ## 1️⃣ What is the DOM? When a browser loads HTML, it parses it and creates the **DOM tree** — a hierarchy of Node objects: ``` Document └── html ├── head │ └── title (#text: "My App") └── body ├── h1 (#text: "Hello World") └── div.container └── p (#text: "A paragraph") ``` JavaScript can access and modify any node in this tree. --- ## 2️⃣ Selecting Elements ```javascript // Single element selectors (return first match or null) document.getElementById("myId") // By ID document.querySelector(".card") // By CSS selector (most versatile) document.querySelector("#nav > a:first-child") // Multiple element selectors (return NodeList) document.querySelectorAll(".card") // All matching elements (like an array) document.getElementsByClassName("card") // Live HTMLCollection document.getElementsByTagName("p") // All

elements ``` --- ## 3️⃣ Modifying Content ```javascript const heading = document.querySelector("h1") // Text content (safe — no HTML injection) heading.textContent = "New Title" // HTML content (allows HTML injection — be careful with user input!) heading.innerHTML = "Bold Title" heading.innerHTML += " (updated)" // Value (for form inputs) const input = document.querySelector("input") input.value = "Default text" console.log(input.value) // Read value ``` --- ## 4️⃣ Modifying Classes & Styles ```javascript const el = document.querySelector(".card") // Classes (preferred over inline styles) el.classList.add("active") el.classList.remove("hidden") el.classList.toggle("dark-mode") // Add if absent, remove if present el.classList.contains("active") // Returns boolean // Inline styles (for dynamic values) el.style.backgroundColor = "#6366f1" el.style.transform = "translateY(-10px)" el.style.display = "none" ``` --- ## 5️⃣ Modifying Attributes ```javascript const link = document.querySelector("a") link.getAttribute("href") // Get attribute value link.setAttribute("href", "/new-page") // Set attribute link.removeAttribute("disabled") // Remove attribute link.hasAttribute("required") // Check existence // For data-* attributes const btn = document.querySelector("[data-id]") console.log(btn.dataset.id) // Access via .dataset btn.dataset.id = "456" // Set via .dataset ``` --- ## 6️⃣ Creating & Inserting Elements ```javascript // Create a new element const newCard = document.createElement("div") newCard.classList.add("card") newCard.textContent = "I'm a new card!" // Insert into the DOM const container = document.querySelector(".container") container.appendChild(newCard) // Add as last child container.prepend(newCard) // Add as first child container.insertBefore(newCard, refNode) // Before a specific node container.insertAdjacentHTML("beforeend", "

Fast insert

") // HTML string // Modern methods (most versatile) container.append(newCard) // Can also append strings refNode.before(newCard) // Insert before this node refNode.after(newCard) // Insert after this node ``` --- ## 7️⃣ Removing Elements ```javascript const card = document.querySelector(".card") card.remove() // Remove itself from the DOM // Old way card.parentNode.removeChild(card) ``` --- ## 8️⃣ Event Listeners ```javascript const button = document.querySelector("#submit-btn") // Adding events button.addEventListener("click", function(event) { console.log("Clicked!", event) event.preventDefault() // Stop default action (e.g., form submit) }) // Arrow function syntax button.addEventListener("click", (e) => { e.stopPropagation() // Stop event from bubbling up the DOM tree }) // Removing events (requires named function) function handleClick(e) { ... } button.addEventListener("click", handleClick) button.removeEventListener("click", handleClick) ``` **Common events:** `click`, `dblclick`, `mouseover`, `mouseout`, `keydown`, `keyup`, `change`, `input`, `submit`, `load`, `DOMContentLoaded`, `scroll`, `resize`. --- ## 9️⃣ Event Bubbling & Delegation Events **bubble up** the DOM tree — a click on a `

questions.question_text: What is the modern way to attach a click event to an element in JS?
questions.explanation: addEventListener is the modern standard, keeping your HTML clean and allowing multiple listeners on one element.
The DOM is a programming interface for HTML and XML documents.

questions.question_text: Which method is the most efficient way to select a single element by its ID?
questions.explanation: getElementById is highly optimized as IDs must be unique in a document.

questions.question_text: How do you change the text content of an element?
questions.explanation: textContent is safer for plain text, while innerHTML allows HTML tags.

questions.question_text: Which method is used to listen for a user clicking a button?
questions.explanation: addEventListener is the standard way to attach event handlers.

questions.question_text: How do you add a CSS class 'active' to an element?
questions.explanation: classList provides convenient methods like add, remove, and toggle.

options: When an event disappears after being called
When an event fires on a child element and then moves up to its parents
When an event happens multiple times a second
When the browser reloads after an event
questions.explanation: Events propagate from the inner-most target up through the DOM tree.

questions.question_text: Which property would you use to change the background color of an element directly in JS?
questions.explanation: JS uses camelCase for CSS properties when using the .style object.

questions.question_text: How do you create a completely new <div> element using JS?
questions.explanation: This creates the element in memory; you then need to append it to the DOM.

questions.question_text: Which method adds a node as the last child of a parent element?
questions.explanation: appendChild is older; append is more modern and can handle multiple nodes or strings.

questions.question_text: How do you prevent a form from submitting (reloading the page) after a click?
questions.explanation: preventDefault stops the browser's default behavior for that event.

questions.question_text: What does a 'NodeList' returned by querySelectorAll differ from a normal 'Array'?
options: It's a collection of nodes that doesn't have all array methods like map() or filter() natively (though it has forEach)
It can only be used in CSS
questions.explanation: You often need to convert it to an array using Array.from() to use advanced methods.

questions.question_text: Which property refers to the parent of the current element?
questions.explanation: Both parentNode and parentElement traverse up the tree.

questions.question_text: How do you remove an element from the DOM?
questions.explanation: The modern .remove() method is the easiest way to delete an element.

questions.question_text: What does the 'value' property do on an <input> element?
options: Gets or sets the current text typed by the user
Sets the price of the item
Shows a popup with the text
questions.explanation: Accessing .value is how you process user input in forms.

questions.question_text: Which event fires when the entire HTML document has been fully loaded and parsed?
questions.explanation: DOMContentLoaded is the best time to start running JS that interacts with the DOM.
